Batch 1: Executed batch 1 tasks `T1`, `T4`, and `T5`. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, and a checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/video-editor-timeline-well-20260325-1848/execution_batch_1.json`.

Batch 6: Executed batch 6 task `T10`. Batch 8: Executed batch 8 task `T12`. Resize state is now owned by `useClipResize`, the old `resizeStartRef` threading was removed from timeline data, `useTimelineEditing` consumes the extracted resize hook, `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, and a checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/video-editor-timeline-well-20260325-1848/execution_batch_8.json`.
Batch 9: Executed batch 9 task `T13`. The remaining clip CRUD and library sync logic now lives in `useClipEditing`, the obsolete `useTimelineEditing` hook was deleted, provider composition was updated to import the split hooks directly, `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, and a checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/video-editor-timeline-well-20260325-1848/execution_batch_9.json`.
Batch 10: Executed batch 10 task `T14`. `useTimelineState` already composes `useClipEditing`, `useExternalDrop`, `useAssetManagement`, `useClipResize`, and `useDragCoordinator`, the editor/chrome context surfaces are wired to those hook results, `useTimelineEditing` remains absent, `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, and a checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/video-editor-timeline-well-20260325-1848/execution_batch_10.json`.
Batch 11: Executed batch 11 task `T15` as a final validation pass. TypeScript compiles, the indicator-related `document.createElement` usage is fully gone, `useTimelineEditing` remains deleted, and `drop-target-new-track` is only toggled by `DropIndicator`. During validation I also removed two unrelated `document.createElement` calls and extracted `useTimelineState` types so the provider hook stays under 300 LOC. The plan is not fully green yet: `useTimelineData.ts` still exceeds the 300-line target, and interactive/manual timeline testing could not be completed because the sandbox denied starting the Vite dev server (`listen EPERM`). A checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/video-editor-timeline-well-20260325-1848/execution_batch_11.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: `TimelineEditor` now memoizes cloned rows with `movable: false` on every action and passes that memo to `<Timeline>`. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. By inspection, `useClipDrag` still starts from its capture-phase `pointerdown`, while the library drag gate at `enableDragging={!disableDrag && movable}` is disabled and resize remains enabled via `flexible`.

Added `drop-position.ts` with cached DOM node lookup, shared drop-position math, and extracted drag-kind inference. The shared function computes time, row, target metadata, reject/new-track flags, and screen coordinates without mutating the DOM.

Added a portal-backed `DropIndicator` with an imperative handle and internal state only. It renders the same row/line/ghost/ghost-label/label structure, hides line/ghost/label for new-track drops, and toggles `drop-target-new-track` from `useLayoutEffect`. File length is 110 LOC.

Removed `onActionMoveStart`, `onActionMoving`, and `onActionMoveEnd` from `useTimelineEditing`, the editor context surface, and `<Timeline>`. `onChange` is now explicitly resize-only sync and no longer checks `crossTrackActive`. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes, and `rg -n "onActionMove(Start|ing|End)"` returns no hits in the edited TimelineEditor/useTimelineEditing/useTimelineState surface.

Added `useDragCoordinator` with a stable `coordinator` API, `indicatorRef`, and `editAreaRef`. `update()` computes a shared `DropPosition`, converts it to `DropIndicatorPosition`, and coalesces `show()` calls behind `requestAnimationFrame`; `end()` cancels pending frames, hides the indicator, and clears `lastPosition`. The hook is now instantiated in `useTimelineState` (provider level), not in `TimelineEditor`.

`useClipDrag` now owns both drag refs locally: `actionDragStateRef` is a single `ActionDragState | null`, and cross-track state is internalized as `crossTrackActiveRef`. `actionDragStateRef`, `clearActionDragState`, and `crossTrackActive` were removed from `useTimelineData`, `useTimelineEditing`, `useTimelineState`, `TimelineEditorContextValue`, and `TimelineEditor.tsx`. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes, `rg -n "actionDragStateRef" src/tools/video-editor` only hits `useClipDrag.ts`, and `rg -n "crossTrackActive" src/tools/video-editor` only hits `useClipDrag.ts` via the internal `crossTrackActiveRef`.

`useTimelineState` now retains the `useDragCoordinator(...)` result and exposes `coordinator`, `indicatorRef`, and `editAreaRef` through `TimelineEditorContextValue`. `TimelineEditor` consumes those refs, populates `editAreaRef.current` from `.timeline-editor-edit-area` in a layout effect, renders `<DropIndicator ref={indicatorRef} editAreaRef={editAreaRef} />` alongside `<Timeline>`, and passes `coordinator` into `useClipDrag`. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

`useClipDrag` now calls `coordinator.update(...)` during pointer moves and reads `coordinator.lastPosition` on pointer-up. All local indicator element types and helpers were removed, `drop-target-new-track` class toggling was removed from the hook, and cross-track drop resolution now comes from the coordinator state. The floating drag ghost remains imperative via `createFloatingGhost()` and `cloneNode`. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes, and `rg -n "document.createElement|ensureIndicator|clearDropIndicators|updateDropTarget|getDropStartTime|getTimelineElements|drop-target-new-track" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts` returns no hits.

`useTimelineEditing` now accepts `coordinator`, uses a RAF-coalesced dragover buffer that calls `coordinator.update(...)`, and resolves drop targets from `coordinator.lastPosition` before calling `coordinator.end()`. The old imperative indicator refs/helpers (`dropIndicatorRef`, `dragPreviewFrameRef`, `latestDragPreviewRef`, `getDropPosition`, `getTimelineDomNodes`) and local `inferDragKind` copy were removed. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes, and `rg -n "dropIndicatorRef|dragPreviewFrameRef|latestDragPreviewRef|getDropPosition|getTimelineDomNodes|indicatorRef\\.current\\.show|drop-target-new-track|inferDragKind = useCallback" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineEditing.ts` returns no hits.

Created `useAssetManagement` and moved `registerGenerationAsset`, `uploadImageGeneration`, and `handleAssetDrop` into it. `useTimelineEditing` now consumes those extracted callbacks instead of defining them inline. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes, `rg -n "document\\.|window\\.|querySelector|classList|coordinator|dragOver|DropIndicator|requestAnimationFrame" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts` returns no hits, and the extracted hook stays focused on data/media logic only.

Created `useExternalDrop` containing the coordinator-based dragover, dragleave, and drop adapter. It imports `inferDragKind` from `drop-position.ts`, accepts `coordinator` rather than `indicatorRef`, and uses `registerGenerationAsset`, `uploadImageGeneration`, and `handleAssetDrop` from `useAssetManagement` for drop handling. `useTimelineEditing` now consumes the extracted hook instead of carrying the adapter inline. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes, and `rg -n "onTimelineDragOver|onTimelineDragLeave|onTimelineDrop|externalDragFrameRef|latestExternalDragRef|latestExternalPositionRef|clearExternalDragState" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineEditing.ts` returns only the exported surface plus the hook wiring, not the old implementation.

Created `useClipResize` with local `resizeStartRef` ownership and moved `onActionResizeStart` / `onActionResizeEnd` into it. `useTimelineEditing` now consumes the extracted resize hook, and `resizeStartRef` was removed from `useTimelineData` so resize state is no longer shared through data composition. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes, `rg -n "resizeStartRef|onActionResizeStart|onActionResizeEnd" ...` shows `resizeStartRef` only in `useClipResize.ts`, and `rg -n "coordinator|drag|drop|pointer|document\\.|window\\.|querySelector|classList" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts` returns no hits.

Created `useClipEditing` with the remaining clip CRUD and library sync handlers (`onChange`, `onOverlayChange`, delete/split/mute/text-add flows) and removed `useTimelineEditing.ts`. `useTimelineState` now imports the split hooks directly so the old name is fully gone. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes, `rg -n "useTimelineEditing|UseTimelineEditingResult|UseTimelineEditingArgs" src` returns no hits, and `wc -l src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.ts` reports 246 LOC.

`useTimelineState` now composes `useDragCoordinator`, `useAssetManagement`, `useExternalDrop`, `useClipResize`, and `useClipEditing`, and the editor/chrome context values are typed and wired against those split hook results. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes, `rg -n "actionDragStateRef|crossTrackActive|onActionMove(Start|ing|End)|useTimelineEditing" src/tools/video-editor` only reports the internal `useClipDrag` refs, and `TimelineEditor.tsx` consumes the updated context surface without stale `useTimelineEditing` dependencies.

Validation results: `npx tsc --noEmit` passes; `rg -n "document.createElement" src/tools/video-editor` returns no hits; `rg -n "actionDragStateRef" src/tools/video-editor` only hits `useClipDrag.ts`; `rg -n "useTimelineEditing" src/tools/video-editor` returns no hits; and `rg -n "classList.*drop-target-new-track" src/tools/video-editor` only hits `DropIndicator.tsx`. `useTimelineState.ts` is now 244 LOC and `useClientRender.ts` is 299 LOC after cleanup, but `useTimelineData.ts` remains 678 LOC, so criterion 10 is still unmet. The dev server smoke start failed with `listen EPERM`, so criteria requiring manual interaction validation remain unverified in this sandbox.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Static verification only: `movable: false` is now applied at the Timeline input, so the library path guarded by `enableDragging={!disableDrag && movable}` will not start its drag visual. `useClipDrag` remains wired via capture-phase `pointerdown`, and resize remains independently enabled by `flexible`.

The new shared utility consolidates row-index, time, reject/new-track, and screen-coordinate math used by both drag paths into `computeDropPosition()`. It performs DOM reads only; there are no DOM writes or indicator mutations in the file.

`DropIndicator` renders the row, line, ghost, ghostLabel, and label structure through a portal using the existing CSS classes, is 110 LOC, and owns `drop-target-new-track` toggling via `useLayoutEffect`.

`npx tsc --noEmit` passes after removing the dead move callbacks. `onChange` remains wired on `<Timeline>` and now performs resize-only synchronization without the old `crossTrackActive` early return.

`useDragCoordinator` uses RAF coalescing for indicator `show()` calls, exposes `coordinator`, `indicatorRef`, and `editAreaRef`, and is instantiated in `useTimelineState` rather than `TimelineEditor`. The next batch still needs to thread those refs through context and render `<DropIndicator>` from `TimelineEditor`.

`rg -n "actionDragStateRef" src/tools/video-editor` now returns hits only in `useClipDrag.ts`. `rg -n "crossTrackActive" src/tools/video-editor` also only returns hits in `useClipDrag.ts`, where the state has been internalized as `crossTrackActiveRef`.

`TimelineEditor` now reads `indicatorRef`, `editAreaRef`, and `coordinator` from context, mounts `<DropIndicator ref={indicatorRef} editAreaRef={editAreaRef} />`, and writes the live `.timeline-editor-edit-area` element into `editAreaRef.current`. The shared `coordinator` is present in `TimelineEditorContextValue`, so both `useClipDrag` and the future external-drop wiring can consume the same provider-level coordinator.

`useClipDrag` now routes drag updates through `coordinator.update()` and cleanup through `coordinator.end()`, while still keeping `createFloatingGhost()` as the pointer-specific imperative clone path. `rg -n "document.createElement|ensureIndicator|clearDropIndicators|updateDropTarget|getDropStartTime|getTimelineElements|drop-target-new-track" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts` returns no hits, so the indicator DOM creation path is gone.

The external drag path now uses `coordinator.update()` on dragover and resolves drops from `coordinator.lastPosition` before calling `coordinator.end()`. `rg -n "dropIndicatorRef|dragPreviewFrameRef|latestDragPreviewRef|getDropPosition|getTimelineDomNodes|indicatorRef\\.current\\.show|drop-target-new-track|inferDragKind = useCallback" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineEditing.ts` returns no hits, confirming the old imperative indicator plumbing and local position helpers were removed.

`useAssetManagement` is now a separate hook containing `registerGenerationAsset`, `uploadImageGeneration`, and `handleAssetDrop`. It has no DOM manipulation or coordinator dependency (`rg -n "document\\.|window\\.|querySelector|classList|coordinator|dragOver|DropIndicator|requestAnimationFrame" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts` returns no hits), and all dependencies needed by the extracted callbacks are passed through its args, including the selection/project state they currently rely on.

`useExternalDrop` now receives `coordinator` in its args, imports `inferDragKind` from `drop-position.ts`, and routes drop behavior through the extracted `useAssetManagement` callbacks (`registerGenerationAsset`, `uploadImageGeneration`, `handleAssetDrop`). `rg -n "coordinator|inferDragKind|registerGenerationAsset|uploadImageGeneration|handleAssetDrop|indicatorRef|getDropPosition|getTimelineDomNodes" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts` confirms the new dependency shape, and there is no raw `indicatorRef` usage or local `inferDragKind` copy in the extracted hook.

`useClipResize` now owns `resizeStartRef`, and the hook has no drag state or coordinator dependency (`rg -n "coordinator|drag|drop|pointer|document\\.|window\\.|querySelector|classList" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts` returns no hits). `rg -n "resizeStartRef|onActionResizeStart|onActionResizeEnd" ...` shows the resize ref only in `useClipResize.ts`, with `useTimelineEditing` and `useTimelineState` only consuming the extracted callbacks.

`useClipEditing.ts` is 246 LOC and contains the focused clip CRUD plus resize-sync handlers (`onChange`, `onOverlayChange`, delete/reset/split/mute/text-add). `rg -n "useTimelineEditing|UseTimelineEditingResult|UseTimelineEditingArgs" src` returns no hits, and `useTimelineState.ts` now composes `useClipEditing`, `useAssetManagement`, `useExternalDrop`, and `useClipResize` directly.

`useTimelineState` composes all of the split hooks directly: `useDragCoordinator`, `useAssetManagement`, `useExternalDrop`, `useClipResize`, and `useClipEditing`. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes, `rg -n "actionDragStateRef|crossTrackActive|onActionMove(Start|ing|End)|useTimelineEditing" src/tools/video-editor` shows no stale context fields or old hook references outside `useClipDrag`, and the context consumer surface remains valid across `TimelineEditor.tsx` and the other editor/chrome/playback consumers.

Not all 10 success criteria can be marked passed from this environment. Criteria 1-5 and 7 pass by code and grep validation: there is a single `DropIndicator` system, `document.createElement` is gone from `src/tools/video-editor`, `actionDragStateRef` is internal to `useClipDrag.ts`, `useTimelineEditing` has zero references, and `drop-target-new-track` is only toggled in `DropIndicator.tsx`. Criterion 10 remains unmet because `useTimelineData.ts` is 678 LOC. Criteria 6, 8, and 9 were not manually verified because `npm run dev -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 4173` failed with `listen EPERM`, so no interactive browser validation could be completed in this sandbox.
